nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; tends to be found at moderate to greater depths among dense coral formations

diet.notes: Generally accepts marine fish flesh, crustacean flesh, mysis shrimp, and quality frozen preparations. Has been known to accept a variety of meaty foods once acclimated.

avoidWith: small ornamental crustaceans that fit in its mouth

summary: A small, secretive reef basslet from the Indo-Pacific, generally considered reef-safe and noted for striking orange and purple horizontal striping. Tends to occupy caves and rockwork and may pose a risk to very small ornamental crustaceans.
careNotes: Liopropoma swalesi is generally considered reef-safe and tends to shelter within live rock caves and crevices; providing ample rockwork with hiding spots is strongly advised. Has been reported to potentially threaten very small ornamental crustaceans that fit within its mouth. Sources differ on care level — LiveAquaria rates it moderate while BlueZoo Aquatics rates it beginner; intermediate is a reasonable middle ground given its secretive habits and need for stable reef water quality. Temperature recommendations also varied between sources: LiveAquaria suggests 72–78°F (22–26°C) while FishBase reports a preferred wild range of approximately 26–29°C; a target of 24–27°C is suggested to cover both. Note on family: LiveAquaria lists the family as Grammidae, but FishBase and current taxonomy place the species in Liopropomatidae — the latter is used here. FishBase records a maximum total length of 5.6 cm; hobby sources report up to approximately 3 inches (7.6 cm).

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size, tank size, temperament, and care level. BlueZoo reports 2.5 in adult size (vs LiveAquaria 3 in), 30 gal minimum tank (vs LiveAquaria 10 gal), peaceful temperament (vs LiveAquaria semi-aggressive), and beginner care level (vs LiveAquaria moderate).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (5.6 cm TL), taxonomy (family Liopropomatidae, order Perciformes), preferred temperature range (26.4–28.6°C), and native range (Vanuatu, Philippines, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea). FishBase is authoritative on taxonomy and was used to correct the family name from Grammidae to Liopropomatidae.
